{{nihongo|'''Mefu Shrine'''|売布神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

'兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=売布神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町1-1 | 位置={{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月19日|Q2961}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=売布神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''売布神社'''（めふじんじゃ、賣布神社）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}売布山手町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}（高比売神） * 配祀神 - {{jalink|アメノワカヒコ|Q10940674|天稚彦神}} 一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}一族の{{jalink|若湯坐連}}（わかゆえのむらじ）が拠点としていた地であり、本来の祭神は若湯坐連の祖である{{jalink|意富売布連}}（おおめふのむらじ）『高橋氏文』による。『天孫本紀』では、{{jalink|大咩布命}}（おおめふのみこと）。と見られる。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}18年（{{jalink|610年|Q30326}}）の創建と伝える。{{jalink|シタテルヒメ|Q11307233|下照姫神}}は当地の里人が飢えと寒さで困窮しているのを愁い、稲を植え麻を紡ぎ布を織ることを教え、その後豊かになった里人が下照姫神を祀ったという伝承が残る。米谷村の由来もこの伝承にちなみ、米種（まいたね）か売布谷（めふたに）が転訛したといわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列格している。 中世以降、貴布禰神社や貴布禰明神（貴船大明神）と称していたが、{{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）、{{jalink|寺社奉行|Q1036279}}{{jalink|大岡忠相|Q1054143}}より地誌編纂を命じられた{{jalink|並河誠所|Q6563872}}の調査によって当社が式内社の売布神社に比定され、以降は売布神社と称するようになった。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列格した。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - {{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}13年（{{jalink|1816年|Q6953}}）再建。{{jalink|桧皮葺}}の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。{{jalink|阪神・淡路大震災|Q11392882}}で被災するが修復された。 * 拝殿 * 市杵島社 - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島比売命}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷社}} * 豊玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}陵遥拝所 * 社務所 * 地車庫 === 文化財 === === 宝塚市指定有形文化財 === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|社号標石}}]] * 売布神社社号標石（附：文書2冊）（考古資料） - {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}元年（{{jalink|1736年|Q6989}}）建立。附指定は「賣布社石碑之覚」の原本と写。{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}51年）3月30日指定[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html たからづかの文化財]（宝塚市ホームページ）。。 === 宝塚市指定天然記念物 === * 売布神社社叢 - 1976年（昭和51年）3月30日指定。 === 例祭 === 10月18日・19日に行われる{{jalink|だんじり祭|Q5219928}}は{{jalink|八坂神社 (宝塚市清荒神)||八坂神社}}（{{jalink|宝塚市|Q823967}}{{jalink|清荒神 (宝塚市)||清荒神}}）と共催で、米谷東（売布、米谷）、米谷西（清荒神）の2基の{{jalink|地車|Q11424706}}が宮入りする。 === 所在地・交通 === * 兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1 * {{jalink|売布神社駅|Q6807953}}（{{jalink|阪急宝塚本線|Q3832859}}）から徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) }} * {{jalink|中山荘園古墳|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|高売布神社|Q11669105}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Founded = Traditionally {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} 18th year ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}) | Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 19|Q2961}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{jalink|Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} lineage known as the {{jalink|Wakayue no Muraji}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{jalink|Ofume no Muraji}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{jalink|Ōmefu no Mikoto}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}). According to legend, {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}), a survey conducted by {{jalink|Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872}}, who had been ordered by the {{jalink|Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279}} {{jalink|Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6), it was designated as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 13 ({{jalink|1816|Q6953}}). A {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} structure with {{jalink|cypress bark roofing}}. It was damaged in the {{jalink|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665|Inari Shrine}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{jalink|Danjiri Festival|Q5219928}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)||Yasaka Shrine}} ({{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}{{jalink|Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)||Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{jalink|festival floats|Q11424706}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{jalink|Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953}} on the {{jalink|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{jalink|Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Founded = Traditionally {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} 18th year ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}) | Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 19|Q2961}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{jalink|Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} lineage known as the {{jalink|Wakayue no Muraji}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{jalink|Ofume no Muraji}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{jalink|Ōmefu no Mikoto}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}). According to legend, {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}), a survey conducted by {{jalink|Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872}}, who had been ordered by the {{jalink|Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279}} {{jalink|Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6), it was designated as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 13 ({{jalink|1816|Q6953}}). A {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} structure with {{jalink|cypress bark roofing}}. It was damaged in the {{jalink|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665|Inari Shrine}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{jalink|Danjiri Festival|Q5219928}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)||Yasaka Shrine}} ({{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}{{jalink|Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)||Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{jalink|festival floats|Q11424706}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{jalink|Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953}} on the {{jalink|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{jalink|Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Founded = Traditionally {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} 18th year ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}) | Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 19|Q2961}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{jalink|Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} lineage known as the {{jalink|Wakayue no Muraji}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{jalink|Ofume no Muraji}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{jalink|Ōmefu no Mikoto}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}). According to legend, {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}), a survey conducted by {{jalink|Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872}}, who had been ordered by the {{jalink|Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279}} {{jalink|Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6), it was designated as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 13 ({{jalink|1816|Q6953}}). A {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} structure with {{jalink|cypress bark roofing}}. It was damaged in the {{jalink|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665|Inari Shrine}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{jalink|Danjiri Festival|Q5219928}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)||Yasaka Shrine}} ({{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}{{jalink|Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)||Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{jalink|festival floats|Q11424706}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{jalink|Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953}} on the {{jalink|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{jalink|Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Founded = Traditionally {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} 18th year ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}) | Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 19|Q2961}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{jalink|Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} lineage known as the {{jalink|Wakayue no Muraji}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{jalink|Ofume no Muraji}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{jalink|Ōmefu no Mikoto}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|610|Q30326}}). According to legend, {{jalink|Shitateruhime|Q11307233|Shitateru-hime-no-Kami}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}), a survey conducted by {{jalink|Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872}}, who had been ordered by the {{jalink|Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279}} {{jalink|Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6), it was designated as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 13 ({{jalink|1816|Q6953}}). A {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} structure with {{jalink|cypress bark roofing}}. It was damaged in the {{jalink|Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665|Inari Shrine}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} ({{jalink|1736|Q6989}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{jalink|Danjiri Festival|Q5219928}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)||Yasaka Shrine}} ({{jalink|Takarazuka City|Q823967}}{{jalink|Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)||Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{jalink|festival floats|Q11424706}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{jalink|Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953}} on the {{jalink|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{jalink|Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274}} * {{jalink|Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:宝塚市の建築物]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]] destinations]]
