== name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takarazuka, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] Takarazuka, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:売布 (宝塚市)]]

It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|WD=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|WD=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|WD=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|WD=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|WD=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|WD=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|WD=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|WD=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|WD=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|WD=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|WD=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|WD=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|WD=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|WD=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|qid=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|qid=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|qid=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|qid=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|qid=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|qid=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|qid=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|qid=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|qid=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|qid=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|qid=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|qid=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} * {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shōen Tumulus|Q11364274|qid=Q11364274|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Takamefu Shrine|Q11669105|qid=Q11669105|1=Takamefu Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine (Takarazuka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] == name in kana (P1814) == * めふじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005018705 == Commons category (P373) == * Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市売布山手町1-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/02p_95h {{commons category|Category:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka)}} [[EN:Mefu Shrine]] [[FR:Mefu-jinja]] [[JA:売布神社 (宝塚市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3304192}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Mefu Shrine | Image = [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} Mefu Yamate-chō 1-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|49|02|N|135|21|30|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 18th year ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|qid=Q30326|1=610}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|qid=Q2961|1=October 19}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Mefu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Mefu Shrine''' (めふじんじゃ, 賣布神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mefu Yamate-chō, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity – {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} (Takahi-me-no-Kami) * Associated deity – {{ill|lt=Amenowakahiko|Q10940674|qid=Q10940674|lt=Ame-no-Wakahiko-no-Kami|1=Ame no Wakahiko}} The surrounding area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage known as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayue no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (わかゆえのむらじ). It is believed that the original enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ofume no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのむらじ), the ancestor of the Wakayue no Muraji,According to the “Takahashi-shi Bun.” In the “Tenson Hongi,” this figure appears as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmefu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおめふのみこと).. === History === The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=610|Q30326|qid=Q30326|1=610}}). According to legend, {{ill|lt=Shitateruhime|Q11307233|qid=Q11307233|lt=Shitateru-hime-no-Kami|1=Shitateruhime}} took pity on the local villagers who were suffering from hunger and cold, taught them how to plant rice, spin hemp, and weave cloth, and after the villagers became prosperous they enshrined Shitateru-hime-no-Kami. The origin of the name Kometani Village is also said to derive from this legend, either as a corruption of “mai-tane” (sown rice seed) or “Mefu Valley” (Mefu-tani). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. From the medieval period onward, it was known as Kibune Shrine or Kibune Myōjin (Kifune Daimyōjin). However, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}}), a survey conducted by {{ill|lt=Namigawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who had been ordered by the {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|qid=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|qid=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} to compile regional gazetteers, identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mefu Shrine. Thereafter, it came to be known as Mefu Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was designated as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1816|Q6953|qid=Q6953|1=1816}}). A {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cypress bark roofing|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|qid=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} but later restored. * Worship hall (Haiden) * Ichikishima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Toyotama Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} * Remote worship site for the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * Shrine office * Float storage === Cultural properties === === Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Takarazuka City === [[File:Mefu-jinja (Takarazuka), sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine name stone marker}}]] * Mefu Shrine name stone marker (with: two documents) (archaeological materials) – Erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} ({{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}}). The accompanying designation includes the original and a copy of “Memo on the Stone Monument of Mefu Shrine.” Designated on March 30, {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|qid=Q2480|1=1976}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 51).[http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1003729.html Cultural Properties of Takarazuka] (Takarazuka City website). === Natural Monuments designated by Takarazuka City === * Mefu Shrine grove – Designated on March 30, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Annual festival === The {{ill|lt=Danjiri Festival|Q5219928|qid=Q5219928|1=Danjiri Matsuri}} held on October 18 and 19 is co-hosted with {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Kiyoshikōjin, Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kiyoshikōjin (Takarazuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kiyoshikōjin}}), during which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11424706|qid=Q11424706|1=danjiri}}—one from Kometani East (Mefu, Kometani) and one from Kometani West (Kiyoshikōjin)—enter the shrine precincts. === Location and access === * 1-1 Mefu Yamate-chō, Takarazuka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Mefu Shrine Station|Q6807953|qid=Q6807953|1=Mefu-Jinja Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|qid=Q3832859|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} ===
